The Kardashev Scale. Ranking civilisations by the energy they command. In 1964, Soviet astrophysicist Nikolai Kardashev proposed that all civilisations could be ranked by a single measure — not by their wars, their art, or their gods, but by their energy.

Scroll
I Planetary Civilization
I Planetary Civilization
Type I · Planetary Civilization (2347 CE)

Masters of a World.
Every joule of the planet, commanded.

A Type I civilization has fully harnessed the energy of its planet — every joule that strikes its surface from its star, stored and redirected at will.

This means control over geology and climate. Earthquakes redirected. Volcanoes managed. No natural disaster goes unanswered. The civilization doesn't merely survive on its world — it owns it.

Kardashev described Type I as a technological level close to what was attained on Earth in 1964. In reality, it represents an energy output roughly 100,000 times greater than what we currently produce.

10¹⁶ – 10¹⁷ W
~10,000× more
0.73
~2347 CE
  • Global weather & climate engineering
  • Planetary-scale fusion power networks
  • Full geological management
  • Early interplanetary colonization
  • Antimatter energy research

A planet, no matter how masterfully controlled, is still a speck of dust orbiting a star.

II Stellar Civilization
Type II · Stellar Civilization

They Have Caged a Star.
The full output of a sun, harvested.

A Type II civilization harvests the full energy output of its host star — not merely the light that falls on one planet, but every photon, every particle of solar radiation.

Freeman Dyson proposed in 1960 that a sufficiently advanced civilization would construct a megastructure surrounding the star entirely — a Dyson Sphere. In practice, this would more likely be a vast swarm of orbiting collectors, each capturing stellar energy and beaming it to colonized worlds.

A Type II civilization commands roughly 10 billion times more energy than a Type I — enough to sustain millions of colonized worlds and launch vessels between star systems using directed energy beams.

~4 × 10²⁶ W
Dyson Sphere
10 billion × more
Infrared excess
  • Dyson Sphere / Dyson Swarm megastructure
  • Stellar engines — physically relocating the star
  • Interstellar travel at meaningful fractions of c
  • Star lifting — extracting matter from the star
  • Total solar system colonization

A Dyson Sphere would be detectable from Earth — a star surrounded by one emits infrared but no visible light. We have looked. Tabby's Star remains unexplained.

III Galactic Civilization
Type III · Galactic Civilization

The Galaxy Is Their City.
A hundred billion stars, integrated.

A Type III civilization has extended dominion across an entire galaxy. Every star — potentially hundreds of billions — harnessed and integrated into a civilization-wide energy network.

The energy available is roughly 10 billion times greater than Type II. The numbers become almost theological: 4 × 10³⁷ watts — the combined luminosity of an entire galaxy, channeled and directed by intelligence.

At this scale, the civilization has reshaped the galaxy itself. Stars relocated. Dead stars repurposed. Black holes at the galactic core serve as power stations of unimaginable output.

~4 × 10³⁷ W
100–400 billion
100,000 ly
~40 billion
  • Galactic energy network across billions of stars
  • Wormhole networks & FTL communication
  • Black hole energy extraction (Penrose process)
  • Artificial star creation and stellar manipulation
  • Galaxy-wide computation — simulated realities

And yet — silence. The Fermi Paradox remains unanswered. Either they are too far, too quiet, or the journey from Type I to Type II is the great filter.

IV Universal Civilization
Type IV · Universal Civilization

The Edge of the Imaginable.
Two trillion galaxies. All of it.

Michio Kaku extended the scale to Type IV — a civilization harvesting the energy of the entire observable universe, tapping dark energy and the expansion of spacetime itself across all two trillion galaxies.

Researcher Zoltan Galantai made a chilling observation: a Type IV civilization would be effectively undetectable. Its activities would appear to us as natural laws. We might look at the fine-tuned constants of physics and not realise they are deliberate design.

The universe has been running for 13.77 billion years. If any civilization reached Type III a billion years ago, they have had a billion years to become this. The question is whether we will survive long enough to understand what we are.

~10⁴⁶ W
~2 trillion
13.77 billion yrs
~10²⁴
  • Dark energy & spacetime expansion harvesting
  • Manipulation of physical constants
  • Universe-wide computation substrate
  • Creation and destruction of realities
  • Undetectable by lower civilization types

"The universe is not required to be in perfect harmony with human ambition." — Carl Sagan. We are at 0.73. The journey has barely begun.
